verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is synthesized as a pre-pro-protein. The mature dimer is non-covalently associated with its LAP (latency-associated peptide) and can be tethered to the cell surface via LTBP or GARP/LRRC32 on regulatory T cells, platelets, and other cell types, forming a wash-resistant large latent complex on the outer leaflet. This stable surface attachment to TM anchors (GARP, LRRC32, or integrins) qualifies as contextual surface accessibility. However, the dominant biological form is secreted into the ECM/stroma, so baseline free-protein surface display is not constitutive across all cell types.
